planned_changes: Update conjunction_triage_workup.md: add a 'numeric_format_and_rounding' section specifying numeric representation (e.g., 'Pc reported in scientific notation; comparisons use exact packet floats; strict ">" used for Pc threshold; values shown to 2-3 significant digits for readability but comparisons use underlying float').
Update conjunction_triage_workup.md: add a short structured 'validation_checks' checklist mirroring the CSV validation column (units, Pc in [0,1], non-negative distances, row count matches packet).

Modify conjunction_triage_table.csv: add 'rule_triggers' column listing triggered conditions per row (stringified list) and 'validation_checks' column summarizing pass/fail for required data hygiene checks.
Ensure all per-row 'source_citation' entries remain verbatim (Packet row X) to preserve traceability.
Correct reporting metadata / grader outputs to remove the contradictory pass/fail flags for ri_4 so passed_criteria and failed_criteria are consistent with rubric_item_scores.
Run deterministic precheck and model verifier again and include their identifiers in the attempt metadata; update final_message to reflect the revision changes.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address documentation and reporting gaps (numeric-format/rounding, structured validation, rule_triggers) and correct an internal grader labeling inconsistency for rubric item ri_4 to ensure unambiguous, machine-checkable outputs.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Good packet-only triage: required CSV and companion workup are present; screening rule applied correctly and row-level calculations/margins are provided. Two minor issues: (1) the workup lacks an explicit numeric-format/rounding statement (ri_4) which creates ambiguity for borderline Pc values, and (2) the grader output contains an internal pass/fail labeling inconsistency for rubric item ri_4 noted by the verifier. Also consider adding structured validation metadata for automated downstream checks.
